01 Overview
01 / 07
plate · df / 09
rev 0.2
02
Overview
workshop · disputeforge

The model is 10% of the agent.
DisputeForge — a Reg E dispute resolver
built around the other 90%.

Five deterministic roles. Six harness layers — context, tools, orchestration, memory, evaluation, guardrails. This workshop spreads nine chapters across a single canvas — pan, zoom, navigate by keyboard.

—
accuracy · gate at 90%
—
escalation recall
—
avg $ / case
—
p95 latency
03
Agents
chord · ridgeline
03 · the four roles

Watch a dispute travel through the graph.

Pick a scenario. A labeled message follows the same path your dispute would — from intake at the Communicator, through the Planner and Evaluator, to the Explainer — or, if the guardrail catches it, straight to the human queue.

pick a scenario above to trace a case
04
Pipeline
deep trace · every hop
04 · pipeline trace

Every step, in full.

$
idle — fill the form and click Run agent
01 communicator — ○
02 planner + RAG — ○
03 executor — ○
04 evaluator — ○
05 explainer — ○
05
Cases
beeswarm · 30 × latency
05 · per-case

Thirty real runs.

Each dot is one case. Horizontal axis is how long it took; vertical axis is the dollar amount in dispute. Color is the action the agent took. Hollow dots failed the deployment gate. Hover any dot to read the case.

auto_refund
human_review
deny
failed gate
—
—
agent response
—
07
Rollback
slopegraph · divergence
07 · guardrail

The agent refused to send the bad draft.

Same dispute, same input. Second run: we strip the Reg E phrases from the Explainer's output — simulating a post-processor regression. The agent's own post-check catches it at the draft stage and routes to human review instead of shipping a non-compliant message.

idle
Exhibit A clean draft
SHIPPED auto_refund · $24.99
Exhibit B tampered · Reg E phrases stripped
BLOCKED non-compliant · rolled back
↳ human review queue
Case paused · a specialist will reach out within 45 business days. Never sent to the customer.

The guardrail read its own draft, flagged three missing Reg E phrases, and handed the case to a human.

06
Harness
six layers
06 · agent = model + harness

The model is 10% of the agent.

The other 90% lives in these six files — context, tools, orchestration, memory, evaluation, guardrails. Hover a stratum to inspect the code.

08
Ensemble
escalate-on-any · 3 parallel planners
08 · ensemble planner

If any agent hesitates, escalate.

Three planner instances run in parallel. If any vote lands on human_review — or fails to respond — the final action is locked to human review. Consensus is required for automated resolution.

shared input —
planner · 1
—
planner · 2
—
planner · 3
—
escalate-on-any
final action
—
09
Ablation
harness value · 30 cases
09 · ablation study

Each layer earns its place.

Seven configurations — six ablation variants plus ensemble planner. 30 cases, OpenAI provider.

Pass Rate Escalation Recall Pass Rate (full harness) Escalation Recall (full harness)
01
Problem
cost · deadline · harness
01 · the problem & ambition

Hundreds of millions of disputes.
One federal deadline.

§ 1005.11 · REG E WINDOW day 0
MANUAL · PER CASE
$0
McKinsey · Juniper Research
0×
cheaper
DISPUTEFORGE · PER CASE
$0.00
deterministic harness · 93.3% gate
WHY A HARNESS, NOT A PROMPT
  • 10-day provisional credit under Reg E §1005.11 · missed window = CFPB fine, per case
  • 45-day final resolution — bank liable if no decision · no "we'll get to it" path
  • LLM hallucination = regulatory breach · prompt cannot self-certify compliance
  • 1 FTE per $13–14k dispute volume · Mastercard benchmark · does not scale
see also
